title: Modified Chest Thrusts (Airway Obstruction)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Complete upper airway obstruction due to foreign body.

#### Procedure

##### Conscious

- Have the patient sit or stand if possible with the paramedic positioned behind with arms wrapped around the patient’s chest (see **Figure 1**).

- Grasp hands in a fist with thumb side against mid sternum area.

- Compress forcefully, up to 5 times. Assess the airway after each thrust to determine if the obstruction has been dislodged.

##### Unconscious

- Position the patient supine and kneel alongside chest. 
- A child or infant may be positioned across the knees in a head down, supine position. (see **Figures 2 & 3**)

- Place hands in the mid sternum area, one on top of the other (as if to perform CPR).

- Compress sharply up to 5 times. Assess the airway after each thrust to determine if the obstruction has been dislodged.

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3
